methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
KrattWorks, a Series A-stage frontier-tech company in Defense & Security, is evaluated against a peer universe of 8 private and 2 public comparables in the same sector. The peer set includes later-stage private companies such as Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), and Code Metal ($1.2B), alongside public benchmarks.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields a valuation of $1.0B. This median is derived from the full peer distribution spanning $125M to $3.0B, with five named references clustering at or above $1.0B (Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns). The median captures central tendency across the 8-company peer set while acknowledging substantial dispersion in the comp universe.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850M to $1.1B, centered on $1.0B. The range reflects stage and sector positioning relative to later-stage peers, though a single methodology limits triangulation robustness. Key uncertainties include KrattWorks' growth trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, relative revenue multiples across the peer set, and the comparability of product-market positioning within the fragmented frontier-defense sector. Revenue context ($19.7M) should be reconciled against peer revenue figures to stress-test per-dollar valuations.
